title: how to realize a knowledge inferencing using owl:sameAs?
description:
In terms of OpenLink Virtuoso, I want to realize the knowledge inference based on OWL.
But I don't know how to realize this for the occasion as below:
if in an RDF instance graph，there is a triple <InstanceA> <propertyX> <InstanceB>.
and in an OWL document, there is content as:
<Instance_a> owl:sameAs <InstanceA>.
<Instance_b> owl:sameAs <InstanceB>.

then，what I expect to supplement is:
<Instance_a> <propertyX> <Instance_b>. --I mean I want to retrieve this triple with the activated inference function.
Regarding above, I tried operating like this:

Prepare an OWL document in which I asserted <Instance_a> owl:sameAs <InstanceA>.
And
<Instance_b> owl:sameAs <InstanceB>.
Import this OWL document to Virtuoso with a GRAPH name
<http://sampleForReasoning/ontology> for it.
Prepare another GRAPH named <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> where there is triple that is: <InstanceA> <propertyX> <InstanceB>. And also there are triples including <Instance_a> and <Instance_b>.
Then create an inference rule that reference the <http://sampleForReasoning/ontology> named graph:
rdfs_rule_set ('urn:owl.music', 'http://sampleForReasoning/ontology') ;
Then in the SPARQL interface, I execute:
DEFINE input:inference 'urn:owl.music'
SELECT *
FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/instance>
WHERE {
    ?subject <propertyX> ?object.
}


However, the result returned is only still <InstanceA> <propertyX> <InstanceB>, there is no inferred triple such as <Instance_a> <propertyX> <Instance_b>.
How to solve the problem?
Actually I know how to realize inference in Virtuoso based on T-box (such as classes and properties) to generate latent triples of instances, but here the owl:sameAs is about A-box (instances). Then I just don't know how to deal with it.
poc: SPARQL define input:same-as "yes" SELECT ?s FROM <g-sas> WHERE  {    ?s ?p ?o ; ?p1  "ent1" };
key_bug_features: Inference not triggering for owl:sameAs relationships
bug_feature_seed: SPARQL PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> INSERT DATA { GRAPH <http://sampleForReasoning/ontology> { ex:Instance_a owl:sameAs ex:InstanceA . ex:Instance_b owl:sameAs ex:InstanceB . } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> INSERT DATA { GRAPH <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> { ex:InstanceA ex:propertyX ex:InstanceB . } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?s ?o FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> WHERE { ?s ex:propertyX ?o };
SPARQL DEFINE input:inference 'urn:owl.music' PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?s ?o FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> WHERE { ?s ex:propertyX ?o };
SPARQL DEFINE input:same-as "yes" PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?s ?o FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> WHERE { ?s ex:propertyX ?o };

SPARQL PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> INSERT DATA { GRAPH <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> { ex:InstanceA owl:sameAs ex:Instance_a . ex:InstanceB owl:sameAs ex:Instance_b . } };
SPARQL DEFINE input:same-as "yes" PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?s ?o FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> WHERE { ?s ex:propertyX ?o };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?s ?o FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> WHERE { ?s ex:propertyX ?o FILTER(?s = ex:Instance_a || ?o = ex:Instance_b) };
SPARQL PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?x ?y FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/ontology> WHERE { ?x owl:sameAs ?y };
SPARQL DEFINE input:same-as "yes" PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?s ?p ?o FROM <http://sampleForReasoning/instance> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o };
